Live Scraper

Yellow Pages Search Scraper

Search a category in a location, scrape business listings, and export to JSON.

⚡ Live web data extraction

Turn public web pages into clean datasets

Live Scraper collects businesses, products, reviews and search results from across the open web — routed through rotating proxies, never your own IP — and exports them to JSON or Excel in a single click.

News comes from Google News through the proxy pool (paid PROXY_URL if set, else the free pool — the real IP is never used).
Leave empty for all articles (Google News gives up to ~100 per query).
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
Image results via Bing image search through the proxy pool (DuckDuckGo's image API is blocked and Google Images is anti-bot; Bing is the reliable free source) — the real IP is never used.
Leave empty for all (0 = all).
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
Google video search through your paid residential PROXY_URL (Google blocks free/datacenter IPs); falls back to Bing video search on the free pool. The real IP is never used.
Leave empty for all (0 = all).
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
Google events search through your paid residential PROXY_URL (Google blocks free/datacenter IPs); on the free pool it is often challenged and returns 0. The real IP is never used.
~10 events per page.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One careers.google.com/jobs/results/ search URL per line (a bare keyword also works). Fetched through the proxy pool — paid PROXY_URL if set, else the free pool; the real IP is never used.
Number of jobs to return. Leave empty for all (0 = all).
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One query per line; use term1 | term2 to compare terms. Interest-by-region from Google Trends' free API, through the proxy pool (paid PROXY_URL if set, else free pool); the real IP is never used.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One URL per line. Each page is fetched through the proxy pool (paid PROXY_URL / PROXY_LIST, else free pool — never the real IP), then Claude extracts the data you describe. Needs ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in .env.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One company per line: a kununu.com company URL or a company name. Reviews are read from each company's /kommentare page through the proxy pool (never the real IP). kununu is bot-protected (DataDome) — a residential PROXY_URL is needed for data.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One per line: a producthunt.com/@username URL, an @username, or a bare username. Fetched through the proxy pool (never the real IP). One row per profile.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One per line: a thuisbezorgd.nl restaurant menu URL or a restaurant slug/ID. Fetched through the proxy pool (never the real IP). Thuisbezorgd is Cloudflare-protected — a residential PROXY_URL is needed for data.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One per line: a feefo.com/<locale>/reviews/<merchant> URL or a bare merchant identifier. Uses Feefo's public reviews API through the proxy pool (never the real IP) — works without a residential proxy.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One per line: an angi.com company-list (…/companylist/…) or near-me search (…/nearme/<category>/?postalCode=…) URL. Fetched through the proxy pool (never the real IP). One row per company.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One per line: an offerup.com item-detail, explore, or search URL. Fetched through the proxy pool (never the real IP). One row per listing.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One per line: a 1688.com search URL or a bare keyword. Fetched through the proxy pool (never the real IP). 1688 uses Alibaba's anti-bot (x5sec) — a residential (China-exit) PROXY_URL is needed for data.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One per line: a craigslist.org search URL (…/search/…) or a single listing URL (…/d/…html). Fetched through the proxy pool (never the real IP). One row per listing.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One per line: an allegro.pl listing/category URL (…/listing?string=… or …/kategoria/…) or a single offer URL (…/oferta/…). Fetched through the proxy pool (never the real IP). One row per product. Allegro uses DataDome — live results need a residential proxy.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One per line: an immowelt.de classified-search URL (…/classified-search?…) or a single expose URL (…/expose/…). Fetched through the proxy pool (never the real IP). One row per property. Immowelt uses DataDome — live results need a residential proxy.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One per line: a mobile.de search URL (suchen.mobile.de/fahrzeuge/search.html?…) or a single vehicle details URL (…/details.html?id=…). Fetched through the proxy pool (never the real IP). One row per vehicle. Mobile.de uses Akamai Bot Manager — live results need a residential proxy.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One per line: a willhaben.at search/category URL (…/iad/gebrauchtwagen/… or …/iad/kaufen-und-verkaufen/…) or a single listing URL (…/d/…-<id>). Fetched through the proxy pool (never the real IP). One row per listing. willhaben blocks datacenter IPs — live results need a residential proxy.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One per line: a Feedback Company customer id (e.g. 15626) or a reviews URL (feedbackcompany.com/<locale>/reviews/<slug>). Uses the site's public reviews feed through the proxy pool (never the real IP) — works without a residential proxy.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One per line: a Feedback Company reviews URL (feedbackcompany.com/<locale>/reviews/<slug>). Reads the company profile (rating, review count, contact) through the proxy pool (never the real IP) — works without a residential proxy. One row per company.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One per line: a crunchbase.com/organization/<slug> URL. Fetched through the proxy pool (never the real IP). One row per company. Crunchbase uses Cloudflare — live results need a residential proxy.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One per line: a search term, domain, or URL (e.g. uber / uber.com / https://www.uber.com). Searches Crunchbase through the proxy pool (never the real IP). One row per matching company. Crunchbase uses Cloudflare — live results need a residential proxy.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One per line: a zoominfo.com/c/<slug>/<id> company URL. Fetched through the proxy pool (never the real IP). One row per company. ZoomInfo uses Cloudflare — live results need a residential proxy.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One per line: a deliveroo.co.uk store/menu URL or a bare restaurant id / UUID. Uses Deliveroo's reviews API through the proxy pool (never the real IP). One row per review. Deliveroo uses Cloudflare — live results need a residential proxy.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One per line: a deliveroo restaurant/menu URL (one restaurant) or a restaurant-list/search URL (all listed restaurants). Fetched through the proxy pool (never the real IP). One row per restaurant. Deliveroo uses Cloudflare — live results need a residential proxy.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One per line: an ubereats.com store URL (one restaurant) or a find-near-me / category / city listing URL (all listed restaurants). Fetched through the proxy pool (never the real IP) — works without a residential proxy. One row per restaurant.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One per line: a streeteasy.com for-sale / for-rent search URL. Fetched through the proxy pool (never the real IP). One row per listing. StreetEasy blocks datacenter IPs — live results need a residential proxy.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One per line: a local search (category + city/zip + country), e.g. "bars, NY, USA". Uses Bing Maps' local feed through the proxy pool (never the real IP) — works without a residential proxy. One row per business.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One URL per line. Each page is fetched through the proxy pool (never the real IP) and read by Claude, which extracts the attributes you choose below. Needs an ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in .env.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One per line: a full name followed by the company domain, e.g. "John Doe company.com". Builds the common corporate email patterns, looks up the domain's MX (DNS over HTTPS through the proxy — never the real IP) and SMTP-probes each to return the address the mail server accepts.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One per line: a zillow.com/profile/<agent>/ URL. Fetched through the proxy pool (never the real IP). One row per transaction on the agent's profile. Zillow uses PerimeterX — live results need a residential proxy.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One company per line — a linkedin.com/company URL, a bare slug, or a numeric company id. Read from LinkedIn's public company page through the proxy pool (paid PROXY_URL if set, else the free pool); the real IP is never used.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One company per line — a linkedin.com/company URL, a bare slug, or a numeric company id. Posts are read via LinkedIn's authenticated API (set LINKEDIN_COOKIE = your li_at, best with a residential PROXY_URL); the real IP is never used.
Number of posts to return. Leave empty for all (0 = all).
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One booking.com/hotel/<cc>/<slug>.html URL per line (a bare hotel slug also works). Booking blocks free/datacenter IPs, so a paid residential PROXY_URL is needed; the real IP is never used.
Leave empty for all (0 = all).
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
Re-scans each hotel's reviews on schedule (headless browser via the proxy pool — Booking blocks free/datacenter IPs, so a residential PROXY_URL is best). Emails a report; the real IP is never used.
Reviews scored at or below this (out of 10) are flagged negative.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One booking.com/hotel/<cc>/<slug>.html URL per line (a bare slug also works). Check-in/out dates are added automatically. Rendered via the proxy pool — Booking blocks free/datacenter IPs, so a residential PROXY_URL is needed for prices; the real IP is never used.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One olx.* search URL per line (any country: olx.ro, olx.kz, olx.pl, olx.in…). Fetched through the proxy pool (paid PROXY_URL if set, else the free pool); the real IP is never used.
Leave empty for all (0 = all).
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One per line: a channel URL / @handle / channel id, a playlist URL (?list=), or a plain keyword (searches YouTube). The Video Type toggle (Shorts / Regular) applies to all of them. Uses YouTube's own JSON API (youtubei) — no HTML scraping; goes through a paid PROXY_URL when set.
Leave empty for all (0 = all).
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
One Glassdoor company URL per line — a company Jobs page (…-Jobs-E<id>.htm) or an Overview page (…-EI_IE<id>…). Glassdoor is Cloudflare-protected, so the free pool is often blocked (0 results) until a paid PROXY_URL is set; the real IP is never used.
Leave empty for all (0 = all).
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
How to get Apollo cookies:
1. Install the Cookie-Editor Chrome extension
2. Go to app.apollo.io and log in to your account
3. Click the Cookie-Editor extension icon → Export → Export as JSON
4. Paste all cookies (JSON) below

Finds emails, social links, phones & website meta on each site, through the proxy pool — works on the free pool; the real IP is never used. One row per domain.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
Finds contacts for each company — emails, phone numbers, full names, job titles & social links, through the proxy pool (real IP never used). One row per contact.
Leave empty for all (0 = all).
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
Verifies each email: format, disposable domain, MX (via proxy DNS), then a live SMTP mailbox check → receiving / SMTP validated / Catch All / undeliverable / Cannot validate. SMTP is a direct connection (mail servers reject proxies); if port 25 is blocked it falls back to the domain-level verdict. No mail is sent. One row per email.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
Firmographics from each company's website — public status, employee count, tech-stack signals & site meta, through the proxy pool (real IP never used). Revenue/founding-year need a paid data source and aren't included. One row per company.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
Validates each number and returns line type, carrier, country & location plus E.164/international/national formats — offline via Google's libphonenumber (no network, no proxy). Carrier is best-effort (mainly mobile). One row per number.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
Reviews from Google Play's own internal API (batchexecute) through the proxy pool — works on the free pool, the real IP is never used.

Plain queries
Returns directions between two points. Scrapes Google Maps through your paid residential PROXY_URL in .env (free proxies are blocked). Each time slot is one sampled row (current traffic — per-interval future traffic needs the paid Directions API). No real IP is used.
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
Returns the places from a Google Maps search/place. Scrapes through your paid residential PROXY_URL in .env (free proxies are blocked) — the real IP is never used. Accepts a "category, city" query, a Maps URL, a place_id (ChIJ…), or a feature id (0x…:0x…).
or select a CSV / XLSX / TXT / Parquet file
Returns every review a contributor (Local Guide) has left. Scrapes Google Maps through your paid residential PROXY_URL in .env (free proxies are blocked by Google). No real IP is used.
Reviews per contributor. Leave blank-ish (0) for all.
